Issue: Fix nested Compose map_items in forward and inverse paths
Decision: Make parent's apply_transform skip list expansion when transform is a Compose instance, delegate to Compose's map_items in execute_compose
Rationale: Respect child's map_items setting by letting it handle expansion via its own map_items, avoiding silent override by parent
Author: aymuos15
Description: Nested Compose objects in forward and inverse paths
Risk: Potential silent override of child's map_items by parent's apply_transform

Argument.author: coderabbitai[bot]
Argument.argument: Introduced a module helper `_inverse_one(...)` to centralize per-transform inversion. `Compose.inverse`, `RandomOrder.inverse`, and `SomeOf.inverse` now use `_inverse_one` for inverting contained transforms. `Compose.flatten()` was changed to inline nested `Compose` instances only when `type(t) is Compose` and `t.map_items == self.map_items`, preserving nested `Compose` with differing `map_items`. `apply_transform()` was adjusted so a `Compose` transform can manage list/tuple expansion itself rather than having the caller expand items.

Argument.author: atbenmurray
Argument.argument: Nested Composes have problems that could affect a broader fix and need holistic addressing.
